name: Type.AddTableKey
signature: Type.AddTableKey( table as type, columns as list, isPrimary as logical ) as type
description: Adds a key to the given table type.

name: Type.ClosedRecord
description: Returns a closed version of the given `record` `type` (or the same type, if it is already closed).

name: Type.Facets
description: Returns a record containing the facets of `type`.

name: Type.ForFunction
signature: Type.ForFunction(signature as record, min as number) as type
description: Creates a `function type` from `signature`, a record of `ReturnType` and `Parameters`, and `min`, the minimum number of arguments required to invoke the function.

examples.usage: Type.ForFunction([ReturnType = type number, Parameters = [X = type number]], 1)
examples.output: type function (X as number) as number

name: Type.ForRecord
signature: Type.ForRecord(fields as record, open as logical) as type
description: Returns a type that represents records with specific type constraints on fields.

examples.usage:
let
    columnNames = {"Name", "Score"},
    columnTypes = {type text, type number},
    rowColumnTypes = List.Transform(columnTypes, (t) => [Type = t, Optional = false]),
    rowType = Type.ForRecord(Record.FromList(rowColumnTypes, columnNames), false)
in
    #table(type table rowType, {{"Betty", 90.3}, {"Carl", 89.5}})

name: Type.FunctionParameters
description: Returns a record with field values set to the name of the parameters of `type`, and their values set to their corresponding types.

examples.usage: Type.FunctionParameters(type function (x as number, y as text) as any)

name: Type.FunctionRequiredParameters
description: Returns a number indicating the minimum number of parameters required to invoke the input `type` of function.

examples.usage: Type.FunctionRequiredParameters(type function (x as number, optional y as text) as any)

name: Type.FunctionReturn
description: Returns a type returned by a function `type`.

name: Type.Is
signature: Type.Is(type1 as type, type2 as type) as logical
description: Determines if a value of `type1` is always compatible with `type2`. Parameter `type2` should be a primitive (or nullable primitive) type value. Otherwise, this function's behavior is undefined and shouldn't be relied on.

name: Type.IsNullable
description: Returns `true` if a type is a `nullable` type; otherwise, `false`.

name: Type.IsOpenRecord
description: Returns a `logical` indicating whether a record `type` is open.

name: Type.ListItem
description: Returns an item type from a list `type`.

name: Type.NonNullable
description: Returns the non `nullable` type from the `type`.

name: Type.OpenRecord
description: Returns an opened version of the given `record` `type` (or the same type, if it is already opened).

name: Type.RecordFields
description: Returns a record describing the fields of a record `type`. Each field of the returned record type has a corresponding name and a value, in the form of a record `[ Type = type, Optional = logical ]`.

name: Type.ReplaceFacets
signature: Type.ReplaceFacets(type as type, facets as record) as type
description: Replaces the facets of `type` with the facets contained in the record `facets`.

name: Type.ReplaceTableKeys
signature: Type.ReplaceTableKeys(tableType as type, keys as list) as type
description: Returns a new table type with all keys replaced by the specified list of keys. Each key is defined using a record in the following form: * `Columns`: a list of the column names that define the key * `Primary`: `true` if the key is the table's primary key; otherwise, `false` The specified list of keys is validated to ensure that no more than one primary key is defined and that all key column names exist on the table type.

examples.usage:
let
    BaseType = type table [ID = number, FirstName = text, LastName = text],
    KeysAdded = Type.ReplaceTableKeys(
        BaseType,
        {
            [Columns = {"ID"}, Primary = true],
            [Columns = {"FirstName", "LastName"}, Primary = false]
        }
    ),
    DetailsOfKeys = Type.TableKeys(KeysAdded)
in
    DetailsOfKeys

let
    TypeWithKey = Type.AddTableKey(type table [ID = number, Name = text], {"ID"}, true),
    KeyRemoved = Type.ReplaceTableKeys(TypeWithKey, {}),
    DetailsOfKeys = Type.TableKeys(KeyRemoved)
in
    DetailsOfKeys

name: Type.ReplaceTablePartitionKey
signature: Type.ReplaceTablePartitionKey(tableType as type, partitionKey as nullable list) as type
description: Returns a new table type with the partition key replaced by the specified partition key.

name: Type.TableColumn
signature: Type.TableColumn(tableType as type, column as text) as type
description: Returns the type of the column `column` in the table type `tableType`.

name: Type.TableKeys
description: Returns the possibly empty list of keys for the given table type. Each key is defined using a record in the following form: * `Columns`: a list of the column names that define the key * `Primary`: `true` if the key is the table's primary key; otherwise, `false`

examples.usage:
let
    BaseType = type table [ID = number, Name = text],
    AddKey = Type.AddTableKey(BaseType, {"ID"}, true),
    DetailsOfKeys = Type.TableKeys(AddKey)
in
    DetailsOfKeys

name: Type.TablePartitionKey
signature: Type.TablePartitionKey(tableType as type) as nullable list
description: Returns the partition key for the given table type if it has one.

name: Type.TableRow
description: Returns the row type of the specified table type. The result will always be a record type.

name: Type.TableSchema
description: Returns a table describing the columns of `tableType`. Refer to the documentation for [`Table.Schema`](table-schema.md) for a description of the resulting table.

name: Type.Union
description: Returns the union of the types in `types`.
